person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Walter de Lacy 
B: 1166, Ewyas Lacy, Herefordshire, England 
D: February 24, 1241 (74-75) Trim Castle, Meath, Ireland

 Immediate Family: 
Son of Hugh de Lacy, Lord of Meath, Lacy and Rohesia de Monmouth 
Husband of Margaret de Braose, Lady of Trim 

Father of 
Katherine de Lacy; 
Petronilla de Lacy; 
Egidia de Lacy, Lady of Connacht and 
Gilbert de Lacy, of Trim and Ludlow 

Brother of Hugh de Lacy, 1st Earl of Ulster; Elaine (Elena) de Lacy; Alice de Lacy; Robert de Lacy; Gilbert de Lacy; Aegida de Lacy; William de Lacy and daughter of Hugh de Lacy « less Half brother of William Gorm de Lacy; Ysota O'Conner; Thomas de Lacy and Unknown de Lacy
LESS

Walter de Lacy (c. 1172–1241) was lord of Meath in Ireland. He was also a substantial land owner in Weobley, Herefordshire, in Ludlow, Shropshire, in Ewyas Lacy in the Welsh Marches, and several lands in Normandy. He was the eldest son of Hugh de Lacy, a leading Cambro-Norman baron in the Norman invasion of Ireland.

With his father he built Trim Castle (Irish: 'Caisletheán Bhaile Atha Troim) in Trim, County Meath.
During the revolt of Prince John Lackland, Lord of Ireland, against his brother, King Richard the Lionheart, in 1193–94, Walter joined with John de Courcy to support Richard. Walter apprehended some knights loyal to John along with Peter Pipard, John's justiciar in Ireland. Walter did homage to Richard for his lands in Ireland in 1194, receiving his lordship of Meath. After mounting the throne of England in 1199, John wrote to his justiciar in Ireland to complain that de Courcy and de Lacy had destroyed John's land of Ireland. Walter had made John his enemy.
In 1203, John granted custody of the city of Limerick to Walter's father-in-law, William de Braose, 4th Lord of Bramber. As de Braose was an absentee, Walter served as de Braose's deputy in Limerick.
In 1206–07, Walter became involved in a conflict with Meiler Fitzhenry, Justiciar of Ireland, and Walter's feudal tenants for lands in Meath; Meiler had seized Limerick. King John summoned Walter to appear before him in England in April 1207. After Walter's brother Hugh de Lacy, 1st Earl of Ulster, had taken Meiler FitzHenry prisoner, John in March 1208 acquiesced in giving Walter a new charter for his lands in Meath. Upon his return to Ireland later in 1208, Walter may have acted as Justiciar of Ireland in lieu of the deposed Meiler fitz Henry. By this time, John had begun his infamous persecution of Walter's father-in-law, de Braose, who fled to Ireland.
On 20 June 1210, King John landed in Crook, now in Co. Waterford, with his feudal levy and a force of Flemish mercenaries; John marched north through Leinster. When John reached Dublin on 27 or 28 June, Walter attempted to throw himself on John's mercy, sending five of his tenants to Dublin to place his lands in Meath back in the king's hand, and disclaiming any attempt to shelter his brother Hugh from John's wrath. John attacked eastern Meath, and was joined by 400 of Walter's deserting followers. John would hold Walter's lands in Meath for five years.

In 1211 Walter erected the castle on Turbet Island in the abortive Anglo-Norman attempt to gain control of West Ulster.
Attempting to secure support in Ireland against the brewing revolt that would lead to Magna Carta, John began negotiations to restore Walter to his lands in Meath in the summer of 1215.
Walter was Sheriff of Herefordshire from 1218 to 1222. In 1230 he joined with Geoffrey de Marisco and Richard Mór de Burgh to subdue Aedh mac Ruaidri Ó Conchobair, King of Connacht.
He was a benefactor to the abbeys of Llanthony and Craswall (Herefordshire) and also founded the abbey of Beaubec in Ireland.

On his death his estate was divided between his granddaughters Margery and Maud.

He married Margaret de Braose, the daughter of William de Braose, 4th Lord of Bramber and Maud de St. Valery and had issue.

1. Pernal de Lacy (c.1201 – after 25 November 1288), married Sir Ralph VI de Toeni, Lord of Flamstead, son of Sir Roger IV de Toeni, Lord of Flamstead & Constance de Beaumont.

2. Egidia de Lacy (also called Gille) who married Richard Mor de Burgh Lord of Connaught and Strathearn. Together they had many notable descendants, including Elizabeth de Burgh, Catherine Parr, Margaret de Clare, the Earls of Ormond, King Edward IV of England, King Richard III of England, and many other British monarchs.

3. Gilbert de Lacy of Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire was taken hostage for his father in August 1215. He predeceased his father before 25 December 1230. Gilbert married Isabel Bigod, daughter of Sir Hugh Bigod, 3rd Earl of Norfolk (Magna Charta Surety) & Maud Marshal. They had 1 son and 2 daughters:
3.1 Walter de Lacy, who married Rohese le Botiller but had no issue. Walter died between 1238 and 1241.
3.2 Margery (Margaret) de Lacy, who married Sir John de Verdun, Lord of Westmeath, the son of Theobald le Botiller, 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland and Rohese de Verdun.
3.3 Maud de Lacy, who married Lord Geoffrey de Geneville, Justiciar of Ireland, the son of Simon de Joinville, Seneschal of Champagne, and Beatrix of Burgundy. Together Geoffrey and Maud had at least three children:
3.3.1. Geoffrey de Geneville (died 1283)
3.3.2. Sir Piers de Geneville, of Trim and Ludlow (1256- shortly before June 1292), who in his turn married in 1283 Jeanne of Lusignan by whom he had three daughters, including Joan de Geneville, 2nd Baroness Geneville.
3.3.3. Joan de Geneville, married Gerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald (died 1287).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter de Lacy Lord of Meath於1172年5月26日出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 米亞斯特利姆特利姆時，他的父親Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath11169歲，母親Rohese de Monmouth27歲。他於1195年11月與Margaret de Braose Lady of Trim在英國賀福爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1241年2月24日在他的家鄉享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lacy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lassy in Calvados, named from a Gaulish personal name Lascius (of uncertain origin and meaning) + the locative suffix -acum.

variants.name: Lasee
meanings: Probably an altered form of English or Irish   Lacy  .

variants.name: Lacey
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Lacy  .

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Walter de Lacy Lord of Meath was born on 26 May 1172, in Trim, Trim, County Meath, Ireland, his father, Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath, was 11169 and his mother, Rohese de Monmouth, was 27. He married Margaret de Braose Lady of Trim in November 1195, in Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 24 February 1241, in his hometown, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter de Lacy Lord of Meath est né le 26 mai 1172, à Trim, Trim, County Meath, Irlande, son père, Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath, avait 11169 ans et sa mère, Rohese de Monmouth, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Margaret de Braose Lady of Trim en novembre 1195, à Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 24 février 1241, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Walter de Lacy Lord of Meath am 26. Mai 1172 in Trim, Trim, County Meath, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath 11169 und seine Mutter Rohese de Monmouth 27. Er hat im November 1195 in Herefordshire, England Margaret de Braose Lady of Trim geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 24. Februar 1241 im Alter von 68 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Lord of Meathが1172年5月26日にアイルランドコウントイ メアストリムトリムで生まれた時，父Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meathは11169歳，母Rohese de Monmouthは27歳であった。1195年11月にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでMargaret de Braose Lady of Trimと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1241年2月24日に故郷で68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Lord of Meath(이)가 1172년 5월 26일 아일랜드 카운티 미스 트림 트림에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath(은)는 11169세였고, 그의 어머니 Rohese de Monmouth(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1195년 11월 영국 허퍼드실르에서 Margaret de Braose Lady of Trim(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1241년 2월 24일 그의 고향에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.
